page: Amazon Web Services x1.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Xen hypervisor and an x86_64 architecture. It features 128 vCPUs derived from 64 physical cores of an Intel Xeon E7-8880 v3 processor running at 2.3 GHz. The CPU configuration includes hyper-threading, a 45 MB L3 cache, and support for AVX, AES, and FMA instruction sets. It is equipped with 1952.0 GB of system memory, providing 15.25 GB of RAM per physical core.

The instance includes 3840 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the server exhibits a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10%. However, multi-core performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core tests. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, scoring in the top 25% for both cached small-block and uncached large-block read and write operations.

Given its massive memory capacity and high multi-core throughput, the x1.32xlarge is designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. It is highly suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, big data processing engines, and high-throughput static web serving, which also scored in the top 25% during benchmarks. While single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show poor to weak performance, the massive resource density of 1952 GB of RAM and local NVMe SSDs offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require large datasets to reside entirely in memory.
description: Amazon Web Services x1.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. Running on the Xen hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, it features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores of an Intel Xeon E7-8880 v3 processor clocked at 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 1952.0 GB of RAM, 3840 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. Benchmark data reveals a significant performance tradeoff: single-core CPU metrics are weak, placing in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance are strong, ranking in the top 25%. This profile makes the instance highly efficient for large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, and high-throughput static web serving, where massive memory capacity and parallel processing outweigh single-threaded execution speeds.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x1.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 1952 GB of RAM, and 3840 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x1.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 1952 GB of RAM, and 3840 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-capacity memory-optimized platform featuring massive RAM and local NVMe storage for enterprise in-memory databases and parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 1952 GB of system memory for massive in-memory datasets
3840 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-speed local data access
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance in the top 25%
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for consistent data transfer
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and static web serving
